title: Incremental State Machines, Concurrency, Cancellation, and Ownership · Runtime Contract: State Observability
summary: A parser is recursive and side-effectful, multiple goroutines read one connection, cancellation races with response writes, and error paths cannot state which component owns the buffer or close. Planned artifact: Add and verify the state observability increment in explicit protocol state, event, invariant, and ownership model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Record low-cardinality phase, framing source, bytes, duration, terminal reason, peer class, and correlation without raw secrets or attacker-controlled labels.
Arbitrate parse, application, peer, timeout, cancellation, write, and shutdown failures so exactly one terminal decision owns response and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict State Observability · Theory State Observability
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2458 in a multi-tenant intake service near its connection and memory ceilings. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record low-cardinality phase, framing source, bytes, duration, terminal reason, peer class, and correlation without raw secrets or attacker-controlled labels.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the State Observability contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict State Observability · Theory State Observability.

For Predict State Observability · Theory State Observability, keep State Observability connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

steps.title: Build State Observability evidence · Theory State Observability
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-state-observability-l8xd3t" that uses case 7257 to enforce cursor, framing, reader, and terminal ownership invariants. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
